user_input.raw_summary: User requested via speech-to-text: documentation audit, JSON transcript logging, music algorithm doc, full CD-length album, vocoder pitch projection, 64x64 JS grid visualizer, streaming infrastructure, new 30-min MP3, V21 version cut. Multiple speech-to-text artifacts corrected (Jason->JSON, IDN->ID3, etc).
user_input.proofread: Use your steering and triple-check system in the AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Use your gVisor self-cue and pre-initialization and post-execution hooks. Document the entire project and update the documentation as well. Make sure that all documentation is correct with respect to actual program function across all codebases. Create an understandable description of the algorithmic approach to music generation that has been arrived at — do not dumb it down, however avoid using frequency, modulation, and certain descriptions of constructs that would be unfamiliar to people lacking a PhD-level understanding of these concepts. Update AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, pre-initialization hook, and post-initialization hook for session and transcript logging to add the behavior of logging in JSON format the actual transcripts showing each step that you and your sub-agents perform. Generate another new MP3 using an arbitrary random seed. Build a full CD-length album 'In The Beginning Phase 0' with 12-24 tracks, vocoder bookends, source code readings, ID3 metadata. Build a 64x64 JavaScript grid visualizer with play controls for album, 30-min, and infinite stream modes. Enhance streaming infrastructure with note events and bash launch scripts. Provide updates every 2 minutes with CT timestamps.

user_input.raw_summary: User requested: (1) budget estimation facility for analyzing usage dashboard screenshots, estimating burn rate, planning multi-window work, preemptive pause decisions; (2) continued emphasis on pushing early and often for crash resilience; (3) don't repeat same music in subsequent album tracks.
user_input.proofread: It would be interesting to have a facility where if the user gives a screenshot, then you can make an estimation about what the budget burn is going to be leading up to the 5-hour limit as well as the weekly limit, and you can make a call about roughly when you should pause execution if the budget is going to be burned prior to the cutoff. If we had work that anticipated spanning multiple consecutive 5-hour windows, plan for that. Also: push early and often so that we don't lose information along the way. Don't repeat the same music in subsequent album tracks.
